hero.bio1: Hello, my name is Michael Li, a student at the <universityLink>University of Waterloo</universityLink> studying Mathematics with a Computing minor, focused on software engineering.
hero.bio2: My coursework includes algorithms, data structures, linear algebra, object-oriented programming, computer systems, distributed systems, statistics, numerical computation, and databases.
hero.bio3: I have completed 6 internships and nearly 2 years of part-time software development work across industries including computer vision, developer platforms, government, SaaS, payment systems, and infrastructure tooling.

experience.pageDescription: A brief overview of my professional journey and career milestones.

projects.pageDescription: A collection of Projects I have built.

github.title: GitHub Insights
github.description: A Quick Look at Followers, Repositories, and Contributions Activity from last year.

weseek.accomplishment1: Built and improved a user-facing portal for JPNAP, the largest Internet Exchange Service in Japan, enhancing usability and accessibility for end users.
weseek.accomplishment2: Implemented and maintained database migrations and CRUD functionality using Ruby on Rails (ActiveRecord) and MySQL, ensuring reliable data integrity.
weseek.accomplishment3: Designed backend features following MVC principles, improving code maintainability and supporting future feature expansion.
weseek.accomplishment4: Developed responsive frontend components using AngularJS, TypeScript, and Bootstrap, delivering a smooth and intuitive user experience.
weseek.accomplishment5: Improved application performance and responsiveness by contributing to a Single Page Application (SPA) architecture and frontend caching strategies.

everforth.accomplishment1: Contributed to a full-stack enterprise application for standardizing employee health workflows, using Nuxt.js, Vue.js, and TypeScript on the frontend and Java Spring Boot on the backend.
everforth.accomplishment2: Developed serverless REST API endpoints using AWS Lambda, Python, and Node.js to handle data ingestion, transformation, and persistence.
everforth.accomplishment3: Architected and implemented a serverless reporting pipeline using AWS Step Functions and Python, transforming user-submitted data into individualized PDF reports with dynamic charts and scoring logic.
everforth.accomplishment4: Implemented dynamic PDF layout logic using ReportLab, calculating spacing and pagination to support variable-length sections, comments, and category counts across customers.
everforth.accomplishment5: Contributed to backend infrastructure supporting real-time data processing, integrating with existing systems to improve reliability and performance.
everforth.accomplishment6: Participated in peer code reviews, providing feedback to maintain code quality, consistency, and engineering best practices.

kaima.accomplishment1: Contributed to the development of a customer-facing payment portal using React.js, Node.js, and Express, supporting payment flows across Stripe, Alipay, WeChat Pay, Weibo Pay, and PayPay.
kaima.accomplishment2: Implemented GraphQL schemas and queries to fetch and render data dynamically, enabling responsive UI updates across the application.
kaima.accomplishment3: Collaborated with designers, product managers, and engineers to integrate and deploy new features within an agile development process.
kaima.accomplishment4: Wrote and maintained end-to-end and component tests using Cypress and Storybook, identifying and resolving issues to improve reliability and user experience.
kaima.accomplishment5: Improved interaction responsiveness by implementing optimistic UI updates using React Query, reducing perceived latency by 35%.
kaima.accomplishment6: Reduced page load times by 20% introducing aggressive client-side caching with React Query, minimizing redundant network requests.
kaima.accomplishment7: Participated in code reviews, sprint ceremonies, and knowledge-sharing sessions, contributing to team alignment and code quality.

enju.accomplishment1: Developed a full-stack, AI website translation application using Next.js (App Router), React, TypeScript, Gemini API and Firebase.
enju.accomplishment2: Improved page load times by 25% by separating static and dynamic content, leveraging Static Site Generation alongside server-side rendering for faster initial loads.
enju.accomplishment3: Implemented user authentication and data storage using Firebase Authentication and Firestore, supporting secure access and reliable data persistence.
enju.accomplishment4: Designed strongly typed Firestore document models to enforce type safety and data consistency within a schema-less database.
enju.accomplishment5: Contributed to the development of an embeddable, CDN-hosted client script that scrapes page content, invokes AI-based translation services, and dynamically injects translated content back into customer websites.
enju.accomplishment6: Cached translated content in Firestore to reduce repeated AI translation calls, lowering API costs and improving content load times.
enju.accomplishment7: Integrated AI-driven translation using Google Gemini to support automated, on-demand website localization.
enju.accomplishment8: Architected the application using a layered approach to separate concerns across presentation, business logic, and data access, improving maintainability and scalability.
enju.accomplishment9: Collaborated with a senior engineer to design, build, and launch the MVP from scratch within a four-month term, rapidly shipping features using a single Next.js codebase with shared server and client logic.
enju.accomplishment10: Leveraged serverless tooling including Next.js App Router, NextAuth, Firebase, and Firestore to enable rapid development and deployment.
enju.accomplishment11: Maintained technical documentation using JSDoc to support onboarding, code clarity, and long-term maintainability.

ontario.accomplishment1: Developed a full-stack, AI-powered CVE analysis tool using Python, JavaScript, HTML, and Tailwind CSS to streamline vulnerability research, generating AI-assisted summaries and remediation suggestions using LLaMA 3.1–70B.
ontario.accomplishment2: Implemented bulk CVE data ingestion by scraping and aggregating information from NIST NVD and CVE Mitre sources for faster retrieval and analysis.
ontario.accomplishment3: Researched potential AI integrations and automation workflows for tools used by the Operations team, identifying opportunities to improve efficiency and reduce manual work.
ontario.accomplishment4: Built serverless automation using Azure AD app registrations and Microsoft Graph API to provision role-based SharePoint document permissions via OAuth 2.0 client credentials, enabling automated onboarding and access management.
ontario.accomplishment5: Performed manual testing from an end-user perspective to identify functional issues and potential cybersecurity vulnerabilities.
ontario.accomplishment6: Designed and implemented unit tests using C# (.NET Core, NUnit) and E2E tests using Selenium to validate core workflows and improve reliability on important government websites.

musashi.accomplishment1: Contributed to the development of an AI-driven visual inspection system for vehicle components, supporting defect detection and quality assurance.
musashi.accomplishment2: Implemented event-driven, real-time updates using WebSockets and MongoDB Change Streams, enabling instant defect notifications and live visual feedback during inspections.
musashi.accomplishment3: Developed a full-stack analytics application using Vue.js, TypeScript, Python, FastAPI, and Docker to monitor inspection results, system performance, and historical accuracy metrics.
musashi.accomplishment4: Processed and analyzed AI inspection outputs to surface predictions, confidence scores, acceptance thresholds, and relevant quality metrics.
musashi.accomplishment5: Implemented reusable base CRUD routers to standardize API development and improve backend development efficiency.
musashi.accomplishment6: Collaborated with cross-functional teams to integrate software components and meet project timelines.
musashi.accomplishment7: Worked within an agile development process to manage tasks, iterate on features, and deliver milestones on schedule.
musashi.accomplishment8: Implemented integration tests using Postman and integrated them into the CI/CD pipeline using Newman CLI to improve release reliability.

opslevel.accomplishment1: Contributed to the development of OpsLevel, a internal developer portal that enables engineering teams to manage and streamline their service and infrastructures.
opslevel.accomplishment2: Built and maintained frontend features using Vue.js and JavaScript, improving usability and responsiveness across core product experiences.
opslevel.accomplishment3: Implemented backend functionality in Ruby on Rails, designing and consuming GraphQL APIs to support new product features.
opslevel.accomplishment4: Developed features for the flagship AI Maintenance Agent, enabling automated repository maintenance through AI-driven workflows.
opslevel.accomplishment5: Integrated with third-party platforms including GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket to support AI-powered repository maintenance and improve onboarding flows.
opslevel.accomplishment6: Investigated and hotfixed production issues reported by customers, prioritizing fixes to maintain platform stability and reliability.
opslevel.accomplishment7: Wrote unit tests using RSpec and Jest to validate backend and frontend functionality and prevent regressions.
opslevel.accomplishment8: Collaborated in an agile environment, participating in daily standups, code reviews, and sprint planning with cross-functional teams.
